results.title: Senior Data Scientist - ML/AI Digital Workplace Experience - CVS Health | Built In Boston
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist - ML/AI Digital Workplace Experience - CVS Health | Built In Boston
[...]
# Senior Data Scientist - ML/AI Digital Workplace Experience
[...]
CVS Health®,
[...]
by passionate colleagues
[...]
deeply, innovate with purpose, hold
[...]
accountable and prioritize safety
[...]
We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Data Scientist to join our ML Digital Experience & Digital Workplace team. In this role, you will lead the design, development, and deployment of advanced machine learning models and Generative AI (GenAI) solutions that transform the digital workplace experience. You will work at the intersection of data science, AI innovation, and enterprise technology to deliver intelligent products and capabilities that drive productivity, automation, and enhanced user experiences across the organization. This is an opportunity to shape the future of how employees interact with digital tools and platforms by leveraging cutting-edge ML and GenAI technologies to solve complex business challenges at scale.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
Machine Learning & AI Development: Design, build, and deploy production-grade ML models with end-to-end pipelines and MLOps best practices to ensure scalability, reproducibility, and continuous improvement.
[...]
Generative AI & LLMs: Lead the design and deployment of GenAI solutions leveraging LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning to build intelligent assistants, conversational agents, and knowledge retrieval tools.
[...]
Data Analysis & Insights: Analyze large-scale datasets using statistical methods and advanced analytical frameworks to uncover actionable patterns and measure AI/ML impact.
[...]
Digital Workplace & Employee Experience: Partner cross-functionally to deliver high-impact AI/ML use cases—including predictive analytics, anomaly detection, and workflow automation—across enterprise digital workplace platforms.
[...]
Collaboration, Technical Leadership & AI Governance: Serve as a subject matter expert in ML and GenAI, mentoring team members, driving actionable business recommendations, and contributing to responsible AI governance frameworks.
[...]
5+ years of hands-on experience delivering production-level ML/AI solutions with strong expertise in Generative AI, LLMs, RAG architectures, and prompt engineering.
[...]
Advanced proficiency in Python, ML/AI frameworks (scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Hugging Face, LangChain), NLP techniques, SQL, and large-scale data platforms (Databricks, Spark, BigQuery).
[...]
Hands-on experience with MLOps practices (model versioning, CI/CD, experiment tracking, monitoring) and cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and their AI/ML services.
[...]
Strong foundation in statistics, probability, and experimental design with the ability to apply rigorous analytical methods to real-world problems.
[...]
Master's degree or Ph.D. in Data Science, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Mathematics, Computational Linguistics, or a related quantitative field.
[...]
Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, FAISS), AI orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel), and enterprise AI platforms such as Azure OpenAI Service or Azure Machine Learning.
[...]
Knowledge of digital workplace technologies (Microsoft 365, ServiceNow, endpoint management) with experience in enterprise telemetry and application usage analytics.
[...]
Familiarity with responsible AI principles and explainability techniques (SHAP, LIME), with experience building real-time inference systems and deploying models via APIs and microservices.
[...]
Experience with computer vision or multi-modal AI, prior work in healthcare or enterprise IT environments, and relevant cloud AI/ML certifications (Azure AI Engineer, AWS ML Specialty, Google Professional ML Engineer).
[...]
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Mathematics, Computational Linguistics, or a related quantitative field.

# Senior Data Scientist – ML/AI Digital Workplace Experience
[...]
🦌 Connecticut, Illinois, +2 more states – Remote🦌 Connecticut – Remote 🌽 Illinois – Remote 🍂 Massachusetts – Remote 🤠 Texas – Remote
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]
• Design, build, and deploy production-grade ML models with end-to-end pipelines and MLOps best practices to ensure scalability, reproducibility, and continuous improvement. • Lead the design and deployment of GenAI solutions leveraging LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning to build intelligent assistants, conversational agents, and knowledge retrieval tools. • Analyze large-scale datasets using statistical methods and advanced analytical frameworks to uncover actionable patterns and measure AI/ML impact. • Partner cross-functionally to deliver high-impact AI/ML use cases—including predictive analytics, anomaly detection, and workflow automation—across enterprise digital workplace platforms. • Serve as a subject matter expert in ML and GenAI, mentoring team members, driving actionable business recommendations, and contributing to responsible AI governance frameworks.
[...]
### 🎯 Requirements
[...]
• 5+ years of hands-on experience delivering production-level ML/AI solutions with strong expertise in Generative AI, LLMs, RAG architectures, and prompt engineering. • Advanced proficiency in Python, ML/AI frameworks (scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Hugging Face, LangChain), NLP techniques, SQL, and large-scale data platforms (Databricks, Spark, BigQuery). • Hands-on experience with MLOps practices (model versioning, CI/CD, experiment tracking, monitoring) and cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and their AI/ML services. • Strong foundation in statistics, probability, and experimental design with the ability to apply rigorous analytical methods to real-world problems. • Excellent storytelling and communication skills with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics
[...]
machine learning,
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

deeply, innovate with purpose, hold ourselves accountable and prioritize

The Senior Manager, Insights will lead advanced reporting, analytics, and insight generation in support of enterprise decision-making across CVS Health / Aetna. This role is intentionally hands-on and data-forward, with a strong emphasis on reporting (including reporting automation) and translating complex data into clear, actionable insights for senior leaders.
[...]
Generation & Story
[...]
- Translate data into clear narratives, implications, and recommendations for senior leaders and cross-functional partners.
- Synthesize quantitative and qualitative inputs (primary research, AI platforms like Copilot, digital twin/synthetic agent research, operational data, CX metrics) into cohesive insight products.
- Develop executive-ready deliverables (one-pagers, decks, memos) that clearly articulate the “so what.”
[...]
- Serve as a subject-matter expert in reporting tools and data visualization platforms (e.g., Excel, Powerpoint, Qualtrics, Medallia, SharePoint/Smartsheet-connected reporting).
- Partner with data engineering, analytics, and technology teams to improve data feeds, definitions, and reporting reliability.
- Establish best practices for documentation, data definitions, and version control to improve insight consistency across teams.
[...]
- 7-10+ years of experience in insights, analytics, research, or consulting roles.
- Proven ability to deliver executive-ready insights and recommendations.
- Advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, including complex analysis and leadership-level storytelling.
- Strong synthesis, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
- Experience working in large, matrixed organizations with senior leaders.
[...]
- Experience in healthcare, insurance, financial services, or similarly complex, regulated industries.
- Familiarity with customer experience, journeys, or engagement metrics.
- Experience using or piloting AI-enabled tools for research, synthesis, or insight generation.
- Experience managing teams and setting analytics or insight standards at scale.

results.title: Experienced Senior Data Scientist – Enterprise Digital Analytics, AI Modeling & Business Intelligence | Jobflarely
highlights:
Experienced Senior Data Scientist – Enterprise Digital Analytics, AI Modeling & Business Intelligence | Jobflarely
[...]
# Experienced Senior Data Scientist – Enterprise Digital Analytics, AI Modeling & Business Intelligence
[...]
Welcome to arenaflex, where we believe that healthcare should be as unique as you are. At arenaflex, we carry our heart to every moment of your health—delivering enhanced human-driven healthcare for a rapidly changing world. Our mission goes beyond simply providing services; we strive to make healthcare more personal, convenient, and affordable for millions of people across the nation. As part of our Enterprise Digital Analytics (EDA) team, you'll have the opportunity to leverage cutting-edge analytics, experimentation, causal inference, and strategic problem-solving to drive innovation in digital health solutions.
[...]
We are seeking an experienced and driven Senior Data Scientist to join our Enterprise Digital Analytics team. In this role, you will apply advanced analytics, machine learning, and statistical modeling to solve complex business problems and drive the evolution of digitally enabled products at arenaflex. You'll be instrumental in transforming raw data into actionable insights that shape customer experiences, optimize marketing strategies, and improve health outcomes.
[...]
Create and implement algorithms and statistical predictive models to evaluate scenarios and potential future outcomes. You'll determine scientific approaches and modeling techniques that address complex business challenges.
[...]
Conduct investigations of structured and unstructured data to solve diverse and intricate business issues using advanced statistical techniques, mathematical analyses, and deep knowledge of the organization and industry.
[...]
Work closely with colleagues across departments to understand their challenges and goals. Develop predictive modeling, statistical analysis, data reports, and performance metrics that inform strategic decisions.
[...]
Design online experiments (A/B testing), measure results, and optimize for maximum impact. Leverage methodologies like causal inference and Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM) to optimize tests where traditional A/B testing isn't feasible or appropriate.
[...]
Utilize strong expertise in algorithms and predictive models to investigate issues, identify patterns, and recommend data-driven solutions. Demonstrate advanced programming skills to analyze, compare, and interpret large volumes of data in various formats.
[...]
Build personalization capabilities using artificial intelligence to target specific audiences for relevant outreach, improving customer experience and business outcomes.
[...]
A minimum of 3+ years of experience applying modern machine learning techniques to build predictive models for both classification and regression problems.
[...]
3+ years of experience using Python and SQL for data analysis, manipulation, and model development.
[...]
2+ years of experience preparing data for analysis, working with data engineering teams to create modeling datasets, evaluating data satisfaction and quality, and performing feature engineering.
[...]
2+ years of experience applying knowledge of cutting-edge analytics tools and languages to analyze large datasets from various data sources.
[...]
2+ years of experience in experimental design and supervised/unsupervised machine learning approaches.
[...]
- Proficiency in multiple areas of mathematical analysis methods, machine learning, statistical analyses, experimental design, and predictive modeling, with deep specialization in certain areas.
[...]
- Experience with causal inference methodologies and Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM).
[...]
- Background in personalization algorithms and recommendation systems.
[...]
customer attribution models.
[...]
Expert-level skills in Python, SQL, and machine learning frameworks. Familiarity with cloud platforms and big data technologies is a plus.

results.title: Principal Architect - Distributed Systems | Hirequorum
highlights:
# Principal Architect - Distributed Systems
[...]
We’re building a world of health around every individual — shaping a more connected, convenient and compassionate health experience. At CVS Health®, you’ll be surrounded by passionate colleagues who care deeply, innovate with purpose, hold ourselves accountable and prioritize safety and quality in everything we do. Join us and be part of something bigger – helping to simplify health care one person, one family and one community at a time.
[...]
The Distributed Systems Architect is responsible for defining, evolving, and owning the technical architecture of the Vulnerability Services Engine (VSE) platform, the enterprise‑wide vulnerability and exposure management platform at CVS Health.
[...]
This role provides hands‑on architectural and engineering leadership across distributed systems, big‑data platforms, and application architecture, and serves as a technical owner of a mission‑critical, enterprise‑scale system that ingests, processes, and analyzes vulnerability telemetry across the entire technology landscape.
[...]
- Provide technical ownership and architectural leadership for the Vulnerability Services Engine (VSE) platform, including data ingestion, analytics, distributed processing, and application layers
[...]
- Own the technical specifications, data architecture, and evolution of vulnerability and exposure data products
[...]
- Lead distributed systems and big‑data design for large‑scale telemetry ingestion, processing, and analytics using platforms such as Apache Spark, Databricks, and Apache Kafka.
[...]
- Define and implement integration strategies for internal CVS Health platforms and external vendor systems across multi‑cloud environments (Azure, GCP, AWS)
[...]
- Provide hands‑on engineering leadership through code contributions, design reviews, and deep technical troubleshooting
[...]
- Establish and lead performance engineering and observability practices, including throughput optimization, scalability testing, and system tuning
[...]
- Serve as technical lead for vendor evaluations and proofs‑of‑concept, assessing architectural fit, performance, and scalability
[...]
- 10+ years of experience designing and implementing large‑scale distributed systems
- 5+ years of experience with big‑data platforms and high‑volume data integration
- 5+ years of experience building web and service‑based applications
- Hands-on coding proficiency in at least one of Java, Python, Javascript
- 5+ years in senior technical leadership roles such as Architect, Staff Engineer, Principal Engineer, or equivalent, with demonstrated hands‑on ownership
- Proven ability to operate in enterprise‑scale, mission‑critical environments with strong performance and reliability requirements
[...]
- Deep, hands‑on experience with Apache Spark, Databricks and modern data‑processing frameworks
- Experience with high‑throughput telemetry, logging, or analytics platforms
- Strong background in distributed systems performance engineering and observability
- Experience operating in security, vulnerability management, risk, or compliance domains
- Knowledge of Apache Arrow or similar data interchange frameworks
- Hands‑on familiarity with contemporary AI/ML concepts and supporting tooling
- Expert‑level proficiency in Java, Python and ReactJs
- Advanced degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field

Generative artificial intelligence is transforming how we build products, serve clients, and operate at scale. In the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help turn advanced models into dependable, secure, and high-performing production services. You will work with partners across machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering to deliver solutions with clear return on investment. If you enjoy hands-on engineering, real-world constraints, and high-impact delivery, this role is for you. As a Senior Associate, Generative AI Engineer in the Chief Data and Analytics Office, you will help design, build, and support production generative artificial intelligence products and reusable backend application programming interfaces used across the firm. You will combine large enterprise datasets with large language and multimodal models to deliver scalable, measurable solutions. You will collaborate closely with machine learning, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering partners to ensure reliability, performance, and strong operational controls. results.title: CVS launched an AI Learning Academy for its workers. Here's why
highlights:
# CVS launched an AI Learning Academy for its workers. Here's why
[...]
CVS launched an AI Learning Academy for its workers. Here's why
[...]
# CVS launched an AI Learning Academy for its workforce. Here's why
[...]
Artificial intelligence is here to stay in healthcare, and the industry's largest players, like CVS Health, are making huge commitments to the tech.
[...]
But embracing AI requires a workforce that's ready for the revolution. With that backdrop, CVS has rolled out its internal AI Learning Academy, which aims to educate its workforce on practical applications for the technology and how it can impact and improve their workflows.
[...]
The program was built in collaboration between human resources and tech leaders at the company. Greg Karanastasis, senior vice president for talent and development at CVS, told Fierce Healthcare that the aim was to build something bigger than just a training program.
[...]
Another element of the program was to assuage fears among workers that the AI technology was set to replace them. Sherif Kamel, chief digital and technology officer for corporate functions, said in an interview that employees are well aware of layoffs and the role AI may be playing in those.
[...]
CVS has made significant investments in both patient-facing and internal AI deployments, so the employees were seeing the technology reach their workflows, Karanastasis said. Where they were unclear, he said, is how exactly they were supposed to be using it.
[...]
So in rolling out the AI Learning Academy, the training was customized to meet the needs of individual teams to highlight where AI may benefit them specifically and where it fits into their personal day-to-day work. The program also brought in speakers to discuss ethical and safe use of AI, so that workers could get an outside perspective on the topic, Kamel said.
[...]
Karanastasis said that the specific, tailored workshops were paired with training in basic AI literacy as a foundation.
[...]
In addition to making sure different parts of the business went through the program, Karanastasis said it was critical for company leadership to also engage with the AI Learning Academy, ensuring they were aligned with the frontline teams on how CVS is using AI.
[...]
gravitated towards
[...]
Beyond the impact of tech in their day-to-day work at CVS, the academy offers an opportunity for workers to gain skills they can carry with them if they leave the company, too, said Kamel. As AI proliferates, there will be an expectation across the board that workers will have some level of comfort with it, he said.
[...]
"This is part of investing in the team, whether they stay in the same organization, they have mobility or decide to leave the company," he said. "We're trying to build the skills for their life."

- Ensure strong model governance, documentation, and auditability across all ML and GenAI solutions.

- Apply Responsible AI principles including explainability, transparency, data privacy, security, and regulatory compliance, particularly within healthcare contexts.

- Provide guidance on safe, compliant, and ethical use of LLMs and agentic AI across enterprise use cases.

- Work closely with business stakeholders and product managers to translate requirements into clear analytical problem statements, solution designs, and execution roadmaps.

- Present technical solutions, insights, and progress updates to both technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership.
